$comment: Minimal hooks template: plan injection + tool counter. Lightweight, no external dependencies. Good starting point for any project.

$inline-alternative.$comment: If you prefer not to use external scripts, here are the inline equivalents:

hooks.command: if [ -f .planning/task_plan.md ]; then CONTENT=$(head -30 .planning/task_plan.md 2>/dev/null | sed 's/"/\\"/g' | tr '\n' ' '); echo "{\"systemMessage\":\"ACTIVE PLAN: $CONTENT\"}"; fi

CF=/tmp/claude_tc; C=$(cat $CF 2>/dev/null||echo 0); C=$((C+1)); echo $C>$CF; [ $C -eq 40 ] && echo '{"systemMessage":"40 tool calls reached. Consider running /compact or saving state."}'; [ $C -eq 60 ] && echo '{"systemMessage":"60 tool calls reached. Context degradation likely. Save state and start fresh."}'

$notes: Plan injector: reminds Claude of the active plan before every file edit/write.
Tool counter: tracks cumulative tool calls and warns at 40 and 60.
Both scripts are in hooks/scripts/ and can be customized.
No external dependencies (no prettier, no eslint) -- works in any project.
Thresholds: 40 = yellow warning, 60 = red warning. Adjust in tool-counter.sh.
